mainEntity.name: Why does the MdWrk lander compile static HTML?

acceptedAnswer.text: Static HTML makes the primary public content readable before JavaScript, which supports search, accessibility, answer engines, and deployment-independent verification.

mainEntity.name: Should teams choose MdWrk or Obsidian?

acceptedAnswer.text: Teams should evaluate the workflow they need. MdWrk is positioned around package surfaces, static public docs, and governed workspace contracts rather than a note graph as the primary identity.

mainEntity.name: What does local-first mean for MdWrk?

acceptedAnswer.text: Local-first means the product documentation treats local writing and preview as the default mental model, while sync, export, and external integrations are opt-in workflows.

@graph.name: Offline Markdown editor for local-first writing — MdWrk

mainEntity.name: Is MdWrk an offline Markdown editor?

acceptedAnswer.text: MdWrk is designed around offline-capable authoring. Public docs distinguish local writing and preview from workflows that intentionally contact external services.
MdWrk does not make hosted document storage the default. Sync and export are documented as deliberate workflow choices.

acceptedAnswer.text: MdWrk is a Markdown workspace and package platform focused on local-first authoring, preview, theming, extension contracts, and developer-facing documentation.

mainEntity.name: Does MdWrk require client-side rendering for public documentation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The public lander is compiled into static HTML so core public content is readable from the raw HTML response before any JavaScript executes.

mainEntity.name: Does MdWrk upload documents by default?

acceptedAnswer.text: The public documentation should not imply default uploads. It should describe local storage and identify workflows that intentionally use networked services.
